{{神社 |name = 宗像神社 |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[奈良県]][[桜井市]]外山818 |位置 = {{Coord|34|30|39.0|N|135|51|35.0|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[タキリビメ|多紀理毘売命]]
[[イチキシマヒメ|市寸嶋比売命]]
[[タギツヒメ|田寸津比売命]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = [[式内社]]（[[名神大]]3座）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[天武天皇]]朝以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = [[10月19日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=宗像神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宗像神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]外山（とび）にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 [[鳥見山]]（とみやま）の山麓に鎮座する。 == 社名 == [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]以降「春日社」と称されてきたが、[[明治]]8年（[[1875年]]）に復称した。また別に、近世以降は「中島社・中島さん」とも呼ばれている。 == deity == 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に「三座」と記されており、いわゆる[[宗像三女神]]である[[タキリビメ|多紀理毘売命]]、[[イチキシマヒメ|市寸嶋比売命]]、[[タギツヒメ|田寸津比売命]]の3柱を祀る。 宗像三女神は[[宗像氏]]の奉斎神で、『[[日本三代実録]]』[[元慶]]4年（[[880年]]）[[3月27日 (旧暦)|3月27日]]条に、当社は[[左京]]の[[宗像神社 (京都市) |宗像神社]]ともども[[筑前国]]の宗像神社（現[[宗像大社]]。以下同）の「同神別社」であると記され、[[寛平]]5年（[[893年]]）[[10月29日 (旧暦)|10月29日]]の「[[太政官符]]」所引「[[高階忠峯]]等[[解状]]」（『[[類聚三代格]]』所収）にも「（筑前国）宗像大神同神」とある。 == 歴史 == [[伴信友]]は、[[宗形徳善|胸形徳善]]の娘である[[尼子娘]]が、[[天武天皇]]の[[後宮]]に入って[[高市皇子]]の母となった縁によって、同皇子の外戚の氏神として祀られたものであろうと推測しているが、それ以前に宗像氏が[[本貫地]]から奉斎する宗像三女神を分霊・分祀したものともされている[[大和国]]に宗像氏が居住した明徴はないが、尼子娘が天武天皇の後宮に入ったこと、また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』の[[右京]]・[[河内国]]の[[神別|神別氏族]]にも「宗形氏」が挙げられていることから、少なくとも[[平安時代]]初めまでに[[畿内]]に居住する一族がいたことは確かである。なお、『[[日本書紀]]』[[雄略天皇]]9年2月甲子朔（1日）条に「胸方神」が見え、通説ではこれを筑前宗像大社のこととするが、その祭祀で[[采女]]を犯した[[凡河内香賜]]が[[摂津国]][[三島郡 (大阪府)|三嶋郡]]で捕らえられていることから、これは畿内に祀られていた「胸方神」で、当神社のことであろうとの説もあり（[[田中卓]]「ムナカタの神の創祀」1957（『著作集1』[[国書刊行会]]1987 にも所収）、もしそうであれば天武天皇時代を遙かに遡ることになる。。 その後、高市皇子の後裔である[[高階氏]]が[[氏神|氏の神]]として崇め、元慶4年に官社に列し（『日本三代実録』）、翌5年に本社である筑前宗像神社に准じて神主職を置き、高階氏をこれに補任するよう定められ（同年[[10月16日 (旧暦)|10月16日]]の「太政官符」（『類聚三代格』所収））、寛平5年には修理料として近在の徭丁8人を充てる旨の「太政官符」が下された（上述寛平5年「太政官符」）この官符にはその経緯も述べられている。。延喜の制で3座ともに名神大社に列し、[[月次祭|月次]]・[[新嘗祭|新嘗]]の両祭にも与っていた国史大系本『神名帳』は「並名神大。月次。」としてあるが、九条家本・金剛寺本にはこの下に「新嘗」の2文字があるとも注記している（吉田家本も同じ）。月次・新嘗両祭は祈願・奉賽の関係にある不可分のものなので、「新嘗」の2文字を有する両本を是とすべきであるが、その場合両本には「並大。月次。新嘗。」と「名神」2文字を欠いているので、当社が名神祭に与っていたことも疑わしいものとなる。。 以後の由緒は詳らかでないが、南北朝以後は高階氏の後裔[[玉井家 (高階氏)|玉井家]]の家伝に詳しい玉井栄治郎『登美山鎮座宗像神社記』（1958年）及び『桜井町史』（1954年）。。それによれば、[[高階義岑]]が弟の[[西阿 (僧)|玉井勝坊入道西阿]]とともに[[赤尾城]]（現桜井市赤尾）を拠点に[[南朝 (日本)|南朝方]]に与して、[[興国]]2年（[[1341年]]）[[7月3日 (旧暦)|7月3日]]に[[渡辺渡 (武士)|渡辺渡]]と鵄（とび）村（現在の外山一帯）に合戦した際、当社は兵火に罹って焼亡し、近辺の[[社領]]17町も[[興福寺|興福寺領]]となるなど衰退に傾き、興福寺の縁で[[春日明神]]とその若宮が[[勧請]]せられるに及んで、「春日社」と称されるようになり、宗像神社自体はあってなきが状態に至った。そのために[[正平 (日本)|正平]]9年（[[1354年]]）、[[高階忠正]]が自邸内に神霊を遷して、「中島宗像社」と称えて来たが、[[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]）に[[玉井忠滋]]が旧社地に再興し（但し、春日社も併祭）、[[幕末]]には[[鈴木重胤]]の尽力もあって、[[安政]]6年（[[1859年]]）に改めて筑前宗像神社から神霊を勧請し、翌[[万延]]元年（[[1860年]]）には社殿等を復旧した。 明治に入ってまず無格社に指定され、同8年に「宗像神社」に復称、同21年（[[1888年]]）には宗像神を主deityとする体裁に改めて、同40年（[[1907年]]）に村社に昇格した。第二次大戦後は[[神社本庁]]に属している。 === 神階 === 『日本三代実録』に、元慶4年に官社に列し（上述）、翌5年には「従一位勲八等」であったことが見える。 == 祭祀 == 当社の神事は、[[氏子]]各人を単位とする「大頭屋」と、各戸を単位とする「古頭屋」の2つの[[宮座]]によって斎行されている。なお、festival日は正平9年の高階忠正邸遷座に際して、[[3月27日 (旧暦)|3月27日]]から[[9月19日 (旧暦)|9月19日]]に改めたと伝えられるが、現在の祭日は更にそれを新暦に改めたものである。 === 神職 === 天武天皇の時代から高階氏が奉仕してきたといい（上述元慶5年「太政官符」に所引の「高階忠峯等解状」）、以来明治に至るまで同氏が連綿として継いできた。ちなみに、同氏は忠滋以降「玉井氏」を称するようになったという。 == 社殿 == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿}}]] 本殿は三間二間の[[神明造]][[銅板葺]]で棟に[[千木・鰹木]]を置く。本殿をはじめとする社殿は平成21年（2009年）に改築された。 == 境内社 == *春日社 - [[天児屋根命]]・[[武甕槌命]]・[[経津主命]]・'''比売神'''を祀り、本殿右側に[[春日造]]の社殿で鎮座する *春日若宮 - [[天押雲根命]]を祀り、本殿左側に春日造の社殿で鎮座 *六所神社 - [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]・[[天照大神|天照皇大神]]・[[ツクヨミ|月世見命]]・[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊命]]・[[大国主|大己貴命]]を祀る *琴平神社 - [[崇徳天皇]]を祀る 他に、宮谷神社「こども神社」と市杵島神社が鎮座する。 境内入口には「[[能楽]][[宝生流]]発祥の地」の碑がある。 == 交通 == *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[桜井線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄大阪線|大阪線]] [[桜井駅 (奈良県)|桜井駅]]南口から[[奈良交通|奈良交通バス]]で外山下車。 == 周辺 == * [[桜井茶臼山古墳]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *式内社研究會編『式内社調査報告』第3巻 大和国（Ｂ）、皇學館大學出版部、昭和57年 *谷川健一編 『日本の神々－神社と聖地』第4巻大和、[[白水社]]、1985年 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 宗像神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム、「神道・神社史料集成」 * revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|30|39.0|N|135|51|35.0|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}, copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}, previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山}}. == Shrine name == From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in {{ill|Meiji 8|ja|明治8年}} (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of {{ill|Prince Takai|ja|高市皇子}}. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In {{ill|元慶|ja|元慶}} 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. In {{ill|寛平|ja|寛平}} 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} due to conflicts but was later restored in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the {{ill|安政|ja|安政}} period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in 元慶 4 and attained "Jūichi-i Kunhachi-tō" (従一位勲八等) in 元慶 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)]][[Category:Unsorted In {{ill|元慶|ja|元慶}} 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. In {{ill|寛平|ja|寛平}} 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} due to conflicts but was later restored in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the {{ill|安政|ja|安政}} period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in 元慶 4 and attained "Jūichi-i Kunhachi-tō" (従一位勲八等) in 元慶 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)]][[Category:Unsorted {{Shrine |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|30|39.0|N|135|51|35.0|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}, copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}, previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山}}. == Shrine name == From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in {{ill|Meiji 8|ja|明治8年}} (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of {{ill|Prince Takai|ja|高市皇子}}. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In {{ill|元慶|ja|元慶}} 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. In {{ill|寛平|ja|寛平}} 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} due to conflicts but was later restored in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the {{ill|安政|ja|安政}} period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in 元慶 4 and attained "Jūichi-i Kunhachi-tō" (従一位勲八等) in 元慶 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)]][[Category:Unsorted {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|30|39.0|N|135|51|35.0|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}, copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}, previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山}}. == Shrine name == From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in {{ill|Meiji 8|ja|明治8年}} (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of {{ill|Prince Takai|ja|高市皇子}}. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In {{ill|元慶|ja|元慶}} 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. In {{ill|寛平|ja|寛平}} 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} due to conflicts but was later restored in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the {{ill|安政|ja|安政}} period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in 元慶 4 and attained "Jūichi-i Kunhachi-tō" (従一位勲八等) in 元慶 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)]][[Category:Unsorted {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|30|39.0|N|135|51|35.0|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}, copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}, previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山}}. == Shrine name == From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in {{ill|Meiji 8|ja|明治8年}} (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of {{ill|Prince Takai|ja|高市皇子}}. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In {{ill|元慶|ja|元慶}} 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. In {{ill|寛平|ja|寛平}} 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} due to conflicts but was later restored in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the {{ill|安政|ja|安政}} period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in 元慶 4 and attained "Jūichi-i Kunhachi-tō" (従一位勲八等) in 元慶 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)]][[Category:Unsorted revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|30|39.0|N|135|51|35.0|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}, copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}, previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山}}. == Shrine name == From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in {{ill|Meiji 8|ja|明治8年}} (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of {{ill|Prince Takai|ja|高市皇子}}. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In {{ill|元慶|ja|元慶}} 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. In {{ill|寛平|ja|寛平}} 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} due to conflicts but was later restored in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the {{ill|安政|ja|安政}} period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in 元慶 4 and attained "Jūichi-i Kunhachi-tō" (従一位勲八等) in 元慶 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)-qq]] [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|30|39.0|N|135|51|35.0|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}, copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山}}. == Shrine name == From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in {{ill|Meiji 8|ja|明治8年}} (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of {{ill|Prince Takai|ja|高市皇子}}. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In {{ill|元慶|ja|元慶}} 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. In {{ill|寛平|ja|寛平}} 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} due to conflicts but was later restored in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the {{ill|安政|ja|安政}} period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in 元慶 4 and attained "Jūichi-i Kunhachi-tō" (従一位勲八等) in 元慶 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)-qq]] [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|30|39.0|N|135|51|35.0|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}, copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山}}. == Shrine name == From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in {{ill|Meiji 8|ja|明治8年}} (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of {{ill|Prince Takai|ja|高市皇子}}. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In {{ill|元慶|ja|元慶}} 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. In {{ill|寛平|ja|寛平}} 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} due to conflicts but was later restored in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the {{ill|安政|ja|安政}} period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in 元慶 4 and attained "Jūichi-i Kunhachi-tō" (従一位勲八等) in 元慶 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]

and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)]][[Category:Unsorted {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|30|39.0|N|135|51|35.0|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}, copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山}}. == Shrine name == From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in {{ill|Meiji 8|ja|明治8年}} (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of {{ill|Prince Takai|ja|高市皇子}}. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In {{ill|元慶|ja|元慶}} 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. In {{ill|寛平|ja|寛平}} 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} due to conflicts but was later restored in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the {{ill|安政|ja|安政}} period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in 元慶 4 and attained "Jūichi-i Kunhachi-tō" (従一位勲八等) in 元慶 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)]][[Category:Unsorted {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|30|39.0|N|135|51|35.0|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}, copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山}}. == Shrine name == From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in {{ill|Meiji 8|ja|明治8年}} (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of {{ill|Prince Takai|ja|高市皇子}}. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In {{ill|元慶|ja|元慶}} 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. In {{ill|寛平|ja|寛平}} 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} due to conflicts but was later restored in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the {{ill|安政|ja|安政}} period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in 元慶 4 and attained "Jūichi-i Kunhachi-tō" (従一位勲八等) in 元慶 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}, copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山}}. == Shrine name == From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in {{ill|Meiji 8|ja|明治8年}} (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of {{ill|Prince Takai|ja|高市皇子}}. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In {{ill|元慶|ja|元慶}} 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. In {{ill|寛平|ja|寛平}} 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} due to conflicts but was later restored in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the {{ill|安政|ja|安政}} period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in 元慶 4 and attained "Jūichi-i Kunhachi-tō" (従一位勲八等) in 元慶 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|30|39.0|N|135|51|35.0|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}, copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山}}. == Shrine name == From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in {{ill|Meiji 8|ja|明治8年}} (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of {{ill|Prince Takai|ja|高市皇子}}. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In {{ill|元慶|ja|元慶}} 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. In {{ill|寛平|ja|寛平}} 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} due to conflicts but was later restored in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the {{ill|安政|ja|安政}} period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in 元慶 4 and attained "Jūichi-i Kunhachi-tō" (従一位勲八等) in 元慶 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|30|39.0|N|135|51|35.0|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}, copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山}}. == Shrine name == From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in {{ill|Meiji 8|ja|明治8年}} (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of {{ill|Prince Takai|ja|高市皇子}}. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In {{ill|元慶|ja|元慶}} 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}. In {{ill|寛平|ja|寛平}} 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} due to conflicts but was later restored in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the {{ill|安政|ja|安政}} period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in 元慶 4 and attained "Jūichi-i Kunhachi-tō" (従一位勲八等) in 元慶 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of {{ill|Hōshō School|ja|宝生流}} in Noh theater. == Access == * From {{ill|JR|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, disembark at {{ill|Sakurai Station|ja|桜井駅}} and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社調査報告}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)]][[Category:Unsorted {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Myojin Taisha, 3 shrines)

{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} The grounds include a monument marking the origin of and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on
