[[commons:Category:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)]][[ja:宗像神社 (桜井市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11451878]] Wikidata name: Munakata-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai).jpg |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.51083333333333|N|135.85972222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reign of Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture = {{ill|Sakurai|qid=Q127026}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimiyama|qq=|1=Mount Torimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700112}}. == Shrine name == From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in [[:en:1875|Meiji 8]] (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata no Tokuyoshi|qq=|1=Munakata no Tokuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451927}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takai]]. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|qq=|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] due to conflicts but was later restored in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 and attained "{{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|lt=Junior First Rank, Eight class|qq=}}" (従一位勲八等) in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|qq=|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshikunune|qq=|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q91088537}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] The grounds include a monument marking the origin of [[:en:Hōshō (Noh school)|Hōshō School]] in Noh theater. == Access == * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], disembark at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station]] and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reign of Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture = {{ill|Sakurai|qid=Q127026}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimiyama|qq=|1=Mount Torimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700112}}. == Shrine name == From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in [[:en:1875|Meiji 8]] (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata no Tokuyoshi|qq=|1=Munakata no Tokuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451927}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takai]]. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|qq=|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] due to conflicts but was later restored in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 and attained "{{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|lt=Junior First Rank, Eight class|qq=}}" (従一位勲八等) in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|qq=|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshikunune|qq=|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q91088537}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] The grounds include a monument marking the origin of [[:en:Hōshō (Noh school)|Hōshō School]] in Noh theater. == Access == * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], disembark at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station]] and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft調査報告|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research on Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reign of Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimiyama|qq=|1=Mount Torimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700112}}. == Shrine name == From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in [[:en:1875|Meiji 8]] (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata no Tokuyoshi|qq=|1=Munakata no Tokuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451927}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takai]]. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|qq=|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] due to conflicts but was later restored in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 and attained "{{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|lt=Junior First Rank, Eight class|qq=}}" (従一位勲八等) in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|qq=|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga [[sn:commons:Category:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)]][[ja:宗像神社 (桜井市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11451878]] Wikidata name: Munakata-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai).jpg |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.51083333333333|N|135.85972222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reign of Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimiyama|qq=|1=Mount Torimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700112}}. == Shrine name == From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in [[:en:1875|Meiji 8]] (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata no Tokuyoshi|qq=|1=Munakata no Tokuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451927}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takai]]. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|qq=|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] due to conflicts but was later restored in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 and attained "{{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|lt=Junior First Rank, Eight class|qq=}}" (従一位勲八等) in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|qq=|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshikunune|qq=|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q91088537}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] The grounds include a monument marking the origin of [[:en:Hōshō (Noh school)|Hōshō School]] in Noh theater. == Access == * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], disembark at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station]] and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft調査報告|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research on Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reign of Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimiyama|1=Mount Torimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700112}}. == Shrine name == From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in [[:en:1875|Meiji 8]] (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata no Tokuyoshi|1=Munakata no Tokuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451927}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takai]]. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] due to conflicts but was later restored in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 and attained "{{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|lt=Junior First Rank, Eight class|qq=}}" (従一位勲八等) in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshikunune|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q91088537}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] The grounds include a monument marking the origin of [[:en:Hōshō (Noh school)|Hōshō School]] in Noh theater. == Access == * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], disembark at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station]] and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft調査報告|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research on Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} [[sn:commons:Category:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)]][[ja:宗像神社 (桜井市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11451878]] Wikidata name: Munakata-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai).jpg |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.51083333333333|N|135.85972222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reign of Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimiyama|1=Mount Torimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700112}}. == Shrine name == From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in [[:en:1875|Meiji 8]] (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata no Tokuyoshi|1=Munakata no Tokuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451927}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takai]]. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] due to conflicts but was later restored in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 and attained "{{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|lt=Junior First Rank, Eight class|qq=}}" (従一位勲八等) in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社}}: Enshrines Sutoku]] The grounds include a monument marking the origin of [[:en:Hōshō (Noh school)|Hōshō School]] in Noh theater. == Access == * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], disembark at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station]] and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sakurai revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)]][[ja:宗像神社 (桜井市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11451878]] Wikidata name: Munakata-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai).jpg |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.51083333333333|N|135.85972222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reign of Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimiyama|1=Mount Torimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700112}}. == Shrine name == From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in [[:en:1875|Meiji 8]] (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata no Tokuyoshi|1=Munakata no Tokuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451927}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takai]]. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] due to conflicts but was later restored in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 and attained "{{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|lt=Junior First Rank, Eight class|qq=}}" (従一位勲八等) in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, and a "Hime deity" * Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Ameoshikunune|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshikunune|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q91088537}} * Rokusho Shrine: Enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * Kotohira Shrine: Enshrines [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] The grounds include a monument marking the origin of [[:en:Hōshō (Noh school)|Hōshō School]] in Noh theater. == Access == * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], disembark at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station]] and take a Nara Kotsu bus to Toyama Station. == Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Research on Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft調査報告|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research on Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} Vol. 3, Yamato (B), Kōgakkan University Press, Showa 57 * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 4: Yamato,' Hakusuisha, 1985 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=24701 Munakata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University, 21st Century COE Program * [http://www.begin.or.jp/~munakata/jinjya.html Munakata Shrine] - Toyama District Community Center, "Toyama Stories" {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}} served as shrine priests. [[sn:commons:Category:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai)]][[ja:宗像神社 (桜井市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11451878]] Wikidata name: Munakata-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Munakata Shrine (Sakurai).jpg |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[Sakurai City]], Toyama 818 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.51083333333333|N|135.85972222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha, 3 shrines)
Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Before the {{ill|Reign of Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reign of Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], copper-roofed |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = October 19 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Munakata Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Toyama, [[Sakurai City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimiyama|1=Mount Torimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700112}}. == Shrine name == From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in [[:en:1875|Meiji 8]] (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata no Tokuyoshi|1=Munakata no Tokuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451927}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takai]]. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] due to conflicts but was later restored in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 and attained "{{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|lt=Junior First Rank, Eight class|qq=}}" (従一位勲八等) in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} (Myōjin Taisha) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], previously classified as a {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Torimiyama|ja|鳥見山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimiyama|1=Mount Torimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700112}}. == Shrine name == From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], the shrine was referred to as "Kasuga Shrine" but reverted to its original name in [[:en:1875|Meiji 8]] (1875). In later periods, it was also known locally as "Nakajima Shrine" or "Nakajima-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities, collectively known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|田寸津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the influence of {{ill|Munakata no Tokuyoshi|ja|宗形徳善|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata no Tokuyoshi|1=Munakata no Tokuzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451927}}, whose daughter, Amako, became a consort of Emperor Tenmu and the mother of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takai]]. It is believed the shrine was established as a family deity of Prince Takai's maternal lineage, though some sources suggest that the Munakata clan enshrined the three goddesses here earlier.Refer to the detailed studies of the Munakata deity origins in Yamato. The shrine was later associated with the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}}, descendants of Prince Takai, who revered it as their clan shrine. In [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 (880 CE), it was recognized as a government-supported shrine (官社) according to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 5 (893 CE), the shrine received provisions for repair and continued to be closely tied to the Takai lineage.See "Taikami and Regional Shinto Practices." The shrine declined during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] due to conflicts but was later restored in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 18 (1590 CE) by the Takai family. Significant restoration efforts occurred in the [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] period (1854–1860 CE) and again in the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] era (2009). === Divine Rank === The shrine was elevated to government-supported status in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 4 and attained "{{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|lt=Junior First Rank, Eight class|qq=}}" (従一位勲八等) in [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 5 according to historical records. == Rituals == The shrine's rituals are conducted through two traditional {{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}} systems: 1. "Ōtōya," based on individual parishioners 2. "Kotōya," based on households The annual festival is observed on October 19, reflecting changes from the lunar calendar to the Gregorian calendar. === Shrine Priests === From the time of Emperor Tenmu, the {{ill|Takai Clan|ja|高階氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takai Clan|1=Takashina clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673216}} served as shrine priests. Their lineage has maintained this role into modern times, adopting the name "Tamai Clan" in later generations. == Shrine Buildings == [[File:Munakata-jinja (Sakurai), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] The main hall is a three-by-two-bay [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] structure with copper roofing. It was renovated in 2009 during the Heisei period. == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社}}: Enshrines {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}},
